days.title: Seasons of the year

grammar.title: The four seasons and their gender
grammar.explanation:
Russian has four seasons. Each noun has a **gender**, which matters for adjectives later.

- **зима** (winter), **весна** (spring), **осень** (autumn) are **feminine**.
- **лето** (summer) is **neuter** (ends in **-о**).
- **год** (year) is **masculine**.

To say *which season you like*, just use the season word directly: **Я люблю лето** (I like summer). To say something *is* your favourite, drop the verb: **Лето — мой любимый сезон**.

reading.title: Любимый сезон
reading.text: — Какой сезон ты любишь? — Я люблю лето. А ты? — Зима — мой любимый сезон.
reading.translation: "Which season do you like?" "I like summer. And you?" "Winter is my favourite season."

days.speakingPrompt: Say out loud which season is your favourite and which season you do not like, using «Я люблю...» and «... — мой любимый сезон».

days.title: The months (part 1)

grammar.title: Months are masculine; «Сейчас ...»
grammar.explanation:
**All twelve months are masculine** in Russian. Notice that many end in a soft sign **-ь** (январь, февраль, апрель) or a hard consonant (март, май).

To say what month it is right now, use **Сейчас** + the month: **Сейчас март** (It is March now). No verb is needed.

Tip: the months are **not** capitalised in Russian (unlike English).

reading.title: Какой месяц?
reading.text: — Какой сейчас месяц? — Сейчас апрель. — Апрель — это весна! — Да, весна красивая.
reading.translation: "What month is it now?" "It is April now." "April is spring!" "Yes, spring is beautiful."

days.speakingPrompt: Practise saying the current month with «Сейчас ...» and add which season that month belongs to.

days.title: The months (part 2)

grammar.title: The «-брь» months and stress
grammar.explanation:
The last four months all share the ending **-брь** and put the **stress on the last syllable**: сентя́брь, октя́брь, ноя́брь, дека́брь. Say them as a rhythmic group to remember them.

**июнь** and **июль** look almost the same — the only difference is the final letter (**-нь** vs **-ль**). Both are stressed on the last syllable.

Link each month to a season: лето = июнь, июль, август; осень = сентябрь, октябрь, ноябрь; зима = декабрь, январь, февраль; весна = март, апрель, май.

reading.title: Сезоны и месяцы
reading.text: Июнь, июль и август — это лето. Сентябрь — это осень. Декабрь — это зима.
reading.translation: June, July and August are summer. September is autumn. December is winter.

days.speakingPrompt: Name all twelve months in order out loud, then group them into the four seasons.

days.title: Weather basics

grammar.title: Impersonal weather: «Сегодня тепло»
grammar.explanation:
Russian describes weather and feelings with **short impersonal adverbs** — no subject and no verb in the present.

- **тепло** (warm), **холодно** (cold), **жарко** (hot), **хорошо** (good), **плохо** (bad).

Just add a time word: **Сегодня жарко** (It is hot today). These adverbs end in **-о** and never change.

Do not confuse the adverb **тепло** ("it is warm") with an adjective. The adverb is used alone for the general weather; the adjective form agrees with a noun (e.g. **тёплый день**) and comes later.

reading.title: Какая погода?
reading.text: — Какая сегодня погода? — Сегодня тепло и хорошо. — А зимой? — Зимой холодно.
reading.translation: "What is the weather like today?" "Today it is warm and good." "And in winter?" "In winter it is cold."

days.speakingPrompt: Describe today's weather in one sentence using «Сегодня ...» and an impersonal adverb (тепло / холодно / жарко / хорошо / плохо).

days.title: Talking about something: о/об

vocab.exampleEn: We are talking about the weather.
This is a book about winter.
This is a song about love.

grammar.title: Prepositional case with о / об (about)
grammar.explanation:
To say **about** something, use the preposition **о** + the **prepositional case**.

For most nouns, the prepositional ending is **-е**:
- лето → **о лете** (about summer)
- погода → **о погоде** (about the weather)
- фильм → **о фильме** (about the film)

Feminine nouns ending in **-ь** (soft sign) take **-и**: любовь → **о любви**; осень → **об осени**.

Use **об** (not о) before a word starting with a vowel sound: **об осени**, **об апреле**. Otherwise use **о**.

examples.english: We are talking about the weather.
This is a book about autumn.
This is a song about love.

reading.title: О чём фильм?
reading.text: — О чём этот фильм? — Это фильм о любви. — А книга? — Книга о зиме.
reading.translation: "What is this film about?" "It is a film about love." "And the book?" "The book is about winter."

days.speakingPrompt: Say what you are thinking or dreaming about using «Я думаю о...» or «Я мечтаю о...» with a season or a month.

days.title: Talking about people and places

vocab.exampleEn: This is a book about dad.
I am talking about a friend.
This is a film about the city.

grammar.title: о/об with people and pronouns
grammar.explanation:
The same rule (**о + prepositional**) works for people and places. Most nouns take **-е**:
- мама → **о маме**, папа → **о папе**, друг → **о друге**, город → **о городе**, работа → **о работе**.

Personal pronouns have special prepositional forms you simply memorise:
- я → **обо мне** (about me) — note **обо**, a special form before мне
- ты → **о тебе** (about you)

So you can now say who you are talking or thinking about: **Мы говорим о друге**, **Я думаю о тебе**.

examples.english: This is a film about the city.

reading.title: О ком ты думаешь?
reading.text: — О ком ты думаешь? — Я думаю о маме и о папе. — А о работе? — Нет, сегодня я не думаю о работе.
reading.translation: "Who are you thinking about?" "I am thinking about mum and dad." "And about work?" "No, today I am not thinking about work."

days.speakingPrompt: Make two sentences with «Я думаю о...»: one about a person (mum, dad, a friend) and one about a place or work.

days.title: Review: time, weather and talking about things

grammar.title: Recap: о/об, months and seasons

examples.english: It is December now; in winter it is cold.
We are talking about the weather.

reading.title: Разговор о лете
reading.text: — Какой сейчас месяц? — Июнь, это лето. — Сегодня жарко! — Да. Я мечтаю о море. — А я думаю о работе.
reading.translation: "What month is it now?" "June, it is summer." "It is hot today!" "Yes. I am dreaming about the sea." "And I am thinking about work."

days.speakingPrompt: Hold a short solo dialogue: state the current month and season, describe today's weather with an impersonal adverb, then say one thing and one person you are thinking about using «о/об».
